summary: Implemented CLI entry point using Cobra with --lang flag, ~/.claude/ directory validation, i18n initialization, and Bubble Tea program startup. Created cmd/root.go with NewRootCmd, run, prepare, validateLang, getClaudeDir, validateDataDir, and Execute. Created main.go entry point.
keyDecisions: Extracted prepare() from run() to separate testable validation logic from TUI startup
validateDataDir returns error instead of calling os.Exit directly for testability
Permission test skipped on Windows (different ACL model, os.Chmod unreliable)
Added spf13/cobra dependency for CLI flag parsing
run() calls prepare() which returns (claudeDir, error) then starts Bubble Tea program

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: cmd/root.go implements Cobra root command with --lang flag (default zh, accepts zh/en)
~/.claude/ directory existence check: missing -> print error and exit code 1
~/.claude/ permission check: not readable -> print error and exit code 1
Language flag passed to i18n system before UI init
Bubble Tea program started with tea.NewProgram(appModel, tea.WithAltScreen())
CLI integration tests: flag parsing, missing dir error, permission error
go build produces single binary agent-forensic

notes: cmd package coverage is 61% because run() and Execute() launch Bubble Tea/cobra which cannot be unit tested. All testable helper functions have high coverage (validateLang 100%, validateDataDir 83.3%, getClaudeDir 75%, prepare 75%). Overall project test coverage across all packages is well above 80%.
